text: Stepping into the corridor and peering into the glass-walled meeting room, I feel a quiet anticipation mixed with a hint of alienation—the sterility and orderliness of the environment seem to demand clarity, efficiency, and purpose. Entering the R+D exhibition space, surrounded by color, screens, and reflective text, I feel a subtle dissonance: simultaneously visible and unseen. The words speak to my internal state—awkwardness and a longing for connection—mirrored by the clinical transparency of the nearby room. Here, the boundaries between intention and perception blur, prompting me to reflect on how others see me, and how I wish to be seen.
